tagline: Toronto's top pub on the Danforth
ownership_history: Founded 1998, long-time operators Nathan and Nicole Hynes built a loyal neighbourhood following over 10+ years.
Sold in 2020 to 8590 Group, who raised prices and alienated regulars (complaints about $13 drafts, $60 bills for basic orders).
Closed earlier in 2022 after community backlash.
Reopened fall 2022 under new management, appears to have course-corrected.

signature_items: Auld Spot Bacon Double Cheese Burger with hand-cut chips
Deep fried tamari glazed crispy pork belly
menu_highlights.food_philosophy: No manufactured food - all created fresh daily from locally sourced markets. Globally inspired pub fare.
menu_highlights.drinks_program: Extensive gin, bourbon, cocktail and draught beer selection. Craft beer focus with rotating taps including Mill Street, Two Loons, Common Good, Harmon's Non-Alc.
menu_sections: Drinks (gin, bourbon, cocktails, draught, wine, scotch)

pricing.noted_complaints: Some reviewers find pricing high for pub food ($20 burgers + $7.50-$14.50 pints)

hours.note: Check website for full weekly hours

praise: Exceptional burger quality - juicy, well-made, praised across platforms
Fresh oysters and $1 Monday deal are a major draw
Strong craft beer and whiskey selection
Food quality above average pub fare - owner has restaurant industry experience
Globally inspired menu stands out from typical pub grub
complaints: Pricing perceived as high for pub food (burgers at $20, premium pints at $14+)
Food poisoning incident reported (Dec 2024) - group of 4 sick after burgers/mussels/salad
Dark interior lighting noted as negative by some
Staff service quality inconsistent in some reviews
Some found food 'ok but not exceptional' relative to price
Turbulent ownership history damaged trust with some longtime regulars
